case_title: STEVEN GOODMAN VS. THOMAS MCDONALD et al

docket_entries.description: NOTICE TO PLAINTIFF

EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR ORDER EXTENDING BRIEFING SCHEDULE RE JUNE 8, 2007 HEARING RE ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION OR, ALTERNATIVELY FOR ORDER VACATING OR CONTINUING JUNE 8, 2007 HEARING RE ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION ;, POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, DECLARATION FILED BY DEFENDANT MCDONALD, THOMAS
DECLARATION (FURTHER) OF THOMAS MCDONALD IN SUPPORT OF MCDONALD'S RESPONSE TO THE COURT'S ORDER TO SHOW CASUE RE PRELIM INJUNCTION FILED BY DEFENDANT MCDONALD, THOMAS

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. 1.) THE ISSUE OF THE PERMIT IS A DISCRETIONARY ACT AND THERE IS NO EVIDENCE THAT THE ISSUANCE WAS SUBJECT TO ANY CONDITION BINDING ON THE CITY. 2.) THE EASEMENT IS PART OF THE COMMON AREA AND NO VOTE WAS NECESSARY. 3.) EQUITABLE FACTORS WEIGH AGAINST ISSUANCE OF THE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION: PLAINTIFF DELAYED IN FILING SUIT. PLAINTIFF DID NOT SEEK TO ENGAGE IN A.D.R. PLAINTIFF HAS KNOWN OF THE PROSPECT OF CONSTRUCTION FROM THE TIME THE SETTLEMENT WAS ENTERED INTO. CONSTRUCTION IS UNDERWAY AND MCDONALD WOULD INCUR SUBSTANTIAL COSTS IF DELAYED. STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS MAY BAR PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM. COURT FINDS THAT THE DAVIS-STERLING ACT DOES NOT APPLY. COURT HAS RULED ON THE EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS AND THEY WILL BE MADE AVAILABLE THIS AFTERNOON. PLAINTIFF'S ORAL MOTION FOR A TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER/STAY IS DENIED. DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT THOMAS MCDONALD'S DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. DISCUSSED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE COURT WILL ISSUE AN ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE FOR OCTOBER 3, 2007 PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 128.7(2) TO PLAINTIFF AND HIS COUNSEL TO DEMONSTRATE WHY THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT DOES NOT VIOLATE C.C.P. SECTION 128.7(B) (1) (2) AND (3) GIVEN THE COURT'S EXPRESS RULING ON THE MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION THAT PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM IS BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. THE PARTIES ARE TO FILE SIMULTANEOUS BRIEFS ON OR BEFORE SEPTEMBER 26, 2007 TO ADDRESS (1) WHAT EVIDENCE FROM THE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION HEARING, IF ANY, MAY THE COURT CONSIDER IN RULING ON THE DEMURRER, AND, (2) WHETHER THE COURT'S FACTUAL RULINGS IN CONNECTION WITH PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR THE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION BAR PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM BASED ON THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

Off Calendar Sep-12-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT OCEAN PARC VILLAGE HOMEOWNER'S ASSOCIATION'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.
NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT VACATES ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND ORDERS THE FOLLOWING: THE COURT WILL ISSUE AN ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE FOR OCTOBER 3, 2007 PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 128.7(2) TO PLAINTIFF AND HIS COUNSEL TO DEMONSTRATE WHY THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT DOES NOT VIOLATE C.C.P. SECTION 128.7(B) (1) (2) AND (3) GIVEN THE COURT'S EXPRESS RULING ON THE MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION THAT PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM IS BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. THE PARTIES ARE TO FILE SIMULTANEOUS BRIEFS ON OR BEFORE SEPTEMBER 26, 2007 TO ADDRESS (1) WHAT EVIDENCE FROM THE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION HEARING, IF ANY, MAY THE COURT CONSIDER IN RULING ON THE DEMURRER, AND, (2) WHETHER THE COURT'S FACTUAL RULINGS IN CONNECTION WITH PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR THE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION BAR PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM BASED ON THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. (302/PJM/PM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Jun-08-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION.
APPEARANCES: MICHAEL GEVERTZ, ESQUIRE (STEEFEL LEVITT & WEISS, 415.788.0900) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. PLAINTIFF MICHAEL GOODMAN IS PRESENT. CARLOS A. ALVAREZ, ESQUIRE (STEYER LOWENTHAL, ET AL, 415.421.3400) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT McDONALD. STEPHEN M. WILLIAMS, ESQUIRE (415.292.3656((302/PJB/JU) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT OCEAN PARC. KRISTEN A. JENSEN, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY (OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY, 415.554.4615) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT CCSF.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. 1.) THE ISSUE OF THE PERMIT IS A DISCRETIONARY ACT AND THERE IS NO EVIDENCE THAT THE ISSUANCE WAS SUBJECT TO ANY CONDITION BINDING ON THE CITY.
2.) THE EASEMENT IS PART OF THE COMMON AREA AND NO VOTE WAS NECESSARY. 3.) EQUITABLE FACTORS WEIGH AGAINST ISSUANCE OF THE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION: PLAINTIFF DELAYED IN FILING SUIT. PLAINTIFF DID NOT SEEK TO ENGAGE IN A.D.R. PLAINTIFF HAS KNOWN OF THE PROSPECT OF CONSTRUCTION FROM THE TIME THE SETTLEMENT WAS ENTERED INTO. CONSTRUCTION IS UNDERWAY AND McDONALD WOULD INCUR SUBSTANTIAL COSTS IF DELAYED. STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS MAY BAR PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM. COURT FINDS THAT THE DAVIS-STERLING ACT DOES NOT APPLY. COURT HAS RULED ON THE EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS AND THEY WILL BE MADE AVAILABLE THIS AFTERNOON. PLAINTIFF'S ORAL MOTION FOR A TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER/STAY IS DENIED. DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

Application For Determination Regarding Existence Of Procedural Deficiency
Off Calendar Jun-08-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT OCEAN PARC VILLAGE HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION'S APPLICATION FOR DETERMINATION REGARDING EXISTENCE OF PROCEDURAL DEFICIENCY.
APPEARANCES: MICHAEL GEVERTZ, ESQUIRE (STEEFEL LEVITT & WEISS, 415.788.0900) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. PLAINTIFF MICHAEL GOODMAN IS PRESENT. CARLOS A. ALVAREZ, ESQUIRE (STEYER LOWENTHAL, ET AL, 415.421.3400) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT McDONALD. STEPHEN M. WILLIAMS, ESQUIRE (415.292.3656((302/PJB/JU) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT OCEAN PARC. KRISTEN A. RULING: COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: OCEAN PARC'S REQUEST TO DETERMINE "PROCEDURAL REQUIREMENT" IS NOT IN THE PROPER FORM OF A MOTION TO STRIKE AND COURT WILL NOT ADDRESS IT.
